plan: Identify gas B (pungent-smelling, colored) and gas A (colorless) using the molar mass ratio B/A = 1.107, testing common candidates.
Use the known reaction of A + B under radiation to determine poisonous liquid X.
Verify that X + H₂O yields two strong acids in a 2:1 molar ratio.
Check that adding BaCl₂ to that solution gives a precipitate C (identify C).
Identify liquid Y (same elements as X) using the molar mass ratio X/Y = 1.134.

Confirm all clues are satisfied and state the identity of X. --- ## Solution **Step 1 — Identify A and B** Candidate for B: Cl₂ is a yellow-green, pungent, colored gas with M(B) = 71 g/mol. $$M(A) = \frac{M(B)}{1.107} = \frac{71}{1.107} \approx 64 \text{ g/mol}$$ M = 64 g/mol → **SO₂** (sulfur dioxide), which is colorless. ✓ So **A = SO₂**, **B = Cl₂**. --- **Step 2 — Identify X** Under UV radiation (photochemical reaction): $$\text{SO}_2 + \text{Cl}_2 \xrightarrow{h\nu} \text{SO}_2\text{Cl}_2$$ **Sulfuryl chloride (SO₂Cl₂)** is a colorless, poisonous liquid. M = 32 + 32 + 71 = **135 g/mol** ✓ --- **Step 3 — Hydrolysis of X** $$\text{SO}_2\text{Cl}_2 + 2\,\text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow \text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 + 2\,\text{HCl}$$ - Products: HCl and H₂SO₄ — both strong acids. - Molar ratio HCl : H₂SO₄ = **2 : 1** ✓ --- **Step 4 — Precipitate C with BaCl₂** $$\text{Ba}^{2+} + \text{SO}_4^{2-} \rightarrow \text{BaSO}_4 \downarrow$$ **C = BaSO₄** (white precipitate) ✓ --- **Step 5 — Identify Y** Y contains the same elements as X (S, O, Cl) and is also a liquid. $$M(Y) = \frac{M(X)}{1.134} = \frac{135}{1.134} \approx 119 \text{ g/mol}$$ Thionyl chloride **SOCl₂**: M = 32 + 16 + 71 = **119 g/mol** ✓ Ratio check: 135/119 = **1.134** ✓ --- **All clues verified:** | Clue | Satisfied? | |------|-----------| | A colorless, B pungent & colored | SO₂ (colorless), Cl₂ (yellow-green) ✓ | | Reaction under radiation → poisonous liquid X | SO₂ + Cl₂ → SO₂Cl₂ ✓ | | X + H₂O → 2 strong acids (2:1) | 2 HCl + H₂SO₄ ✓ | | BaCl₂ → precipitate C | BaSO₄ ✓ | | Y has same elements, ratio X/Y = 1.134 | SOCl₂, ratio = 135/119 ✓ | FINAL ANSWER SO₂Cl₂ (sulfuryl chloride)

trajectory.thought: The plan already contains the complete solution. Gas B is Cl₂ (M = 71, pungent, yellow-green colored), and gas A is SO₂ (M ≈ 64, colorless). The ratio 71/64 = 1.109 ≈ 1.107 ✓. The reaction SO₂ + Cl₂ → SO₂Cl₂ under radiation gives poisonous liquid X = sulfuryl chloride (SO₂Cl₂, M = 135). Hydrolysis gives H₂SO₄ + 2HCl (2:1 ratio of strong acids), BaCl₂ gives BaSO₄ precipitate, and Y = SOCl₂ (M = 119, ratio 135/119 = 1.134). All clues are satisfied.
A = SO₂ and B = Cl₂. Under UV/radiation: SO₂ + Cl₂ → SO₂Cl₂ (sulfuryl chloride), a colorless poisonous liquid with M = 32 + 32 + 71 = 135 g/mol.
The hydrolysis reaction of SO₂Cl₂ with water is: SO₂Cl₂ + 2H₂O → H₂SO₄ + 2HCl. This produces two strong acids: HCl and H₂SO₄ in a molar ratio of 2:1, which matches the clue exactly.

When BaCl₂ is added to the solution from X + H₂O (which contains H₂SO₄ and HCl), the Ba²⁺ ions react with SO₄²⁻ ions: Ba²⁺ + SO₄²⁻ → BaSO₄↓. BaSO₄ is an insoluble white precipitate. This confirms C = BaSO₄. The HCl present does not form a precipitate with BaCl₂. All clues check out — X = SO₂Cl₂ (sulfuryl chloride).
Y contains the same elements as X (S, O, Cl). M(Y) = 135/1.134 ≈ 119 g/mol. Thionyl chloride SOCl₂ has M = 32 + 16 + 71 = 119 g/mol. Ratio 135/119 = 1.134 ✓. Y = SOCl₂.
